title: The vendor in Hanoi who taught me the word for fish sauce
summary: A short story about a market seller who patiently taught you a single word that opened the rest of the market.
topic: The vendor in Hanoi who taught me the word for fish sauce
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a market vendor or shopkeeper abroad who taught you one local word. Describe the moment and what changed afterwards. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: During my third week in…, I went to the wet market on…
I pointed at a small bottle and asked…
After that morning, I started using the word…

hintVocab.meaningVi: người bán hàng rong / sạp chợ

sampleText: During my third week in Hanoi, I went to the small wet market on Ngo Si Lien street almost every morning. I was living above a tea shop and cooking for myself, but I did not know the names of half the things on the stalls. One Tuesday I pointed at a small brown bottle on the table of a woman with a wide silver bracelet. 'How say this?' I asked. She smiled and said, 'Nước mắm.' I tried to repeat it, but I dropped the tone and it came out flat. She laughed kindly, lifted her chin, and said it again, very slowly, two syllables. I tried again. This time her eyes widened and she clapped once. 'Tốt,' she said. While I was paying, three other customers were listening, and one old man said something I did not understand but everyone laughed. After that morning, I started using the word at every other stall in the market. People stopped treating me like a tourist and started calling me 'em' or 'cháu'. Six months later, when I finally left Hanoi, I went back to her stall with a small bag of oranges. I did not need to say much. I think she already knew.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I went to the small wet market on Ngo Si Lien street almost every morning
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đi chợ tươi nhỏ trên phố Ngô Sĩ Liên gần như mỗi sáng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I was living above a tea shop
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đang ở trọ phía trên một tiệm trà

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I pointed at a small brown bottle
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi chỉ vào một chai nhỏ màu nâu

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a woman with a wide silver bracelet
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một người phụ nữ đeo vòng tay bạc to bản

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I dropped the tone and it came out flat
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đánh rơi thanh điệu và nó phát ra phẳng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: her eyes widened and she clapped once
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mắt cô ấy mở to và vỗ tay một cái
Trong khi tôi đang trả tiền

sampleAnnotations.phrase: People stopped treating me like a tourist
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Mọi người không còn coi tôi như khách du lịch nữa

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I went back to her stall with a small bag of oranges
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi quay lại sạp của cô ấy với một túi cam nhỏ
Tôi nghĩ cô ấy đã biết rồi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for a setting habit
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I was living above a tea shop' dùng past continuous để mô tả một tình huống nền kéo dài trong một quãng quá khứ, không phải hành động ngắn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I was living above a tea shop' uses past continuous to describe an ongoing background situation over a stretch of past time, not a short action.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple sequence with 'then' and 'after'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể một chuỗi sự kiện ('I pointed... She smiled... I tried... She laughed...'), dùng past simple cho mỗi bước. 'After that morning' báo hiệu chuyển sang giai đoạn mới.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When narrating a chain of events ('I pointed... She smiled... I tried... She laughed...'), use past simple at each step. 'After that morning' signals a shift to a new phase.

sampleTranslationVi: Trong tuần thứ ba ở Hà Nội, tôi đi chợ tươi nhỏ trên phố Ngô Sĩ Liên gần như mỗi sáng. Tôi đang ở trọ phía trên một tiệm trà và tự nấu ăn, nhưng tôi không biết tên của một nửa số thứ trên các sạp. Một sáng thứ Ba tôi chỉ vào một chai nhỏ màu nâu trên bàn của một người phụ nữ đeo vòng tay bạc to bản. 'Cái này nói sao?' tôi hỏi. Cô ấy mỉm cười và nói, 'Nước mắm.' Tôi cố lặp lại, nhưng tôi đánh rơi thanh điệu và nó phát ra phẳng. Cô ấy cười hiền lành, ngẩng cằm lên và nói lại, rất chậm, hai âm tiết. Tôi thử lại. Lần này mắt cô ấy mở to và vỗ tay một cái. 'Tốt,' cô ấy nói. Trong khi tôi đang trả tiền, ba khách khác lắng nghe, và một ông cụ nói gì đó tôi không hiểu nhưng mọi người cười. Sau buổi sáng đó, tôi bắt đầu dùng từ này ở hầu hết các sạp khác trong chợ. Mọi người không còn coi tôi như khách du lịch nữa và bắt đầu gọi tôi là 'em' hoặc 'cháu'. Sáu tháng sau, khi cuối cùng tôi rời Hà Nội, tôi quay lại sạp của cô ấy với một túi cam nhỏ. Tôi không cần nói nhiều. Tôi nghĩ cô ấy đã biết rồi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past continuous sentence.
options: I was living above a tea shop.
I am live above a tea shop.
I have lived above a tea shop.
I live above a tea shop.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was living' for an ongoing past situation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past simple chain.
options: She smile and say, 'Nước mắm.'
She smiled and said, 'Nước mắm.'
She smiles and say, 'Nước mắm.'
She was smiling and was saying, 'Nước mắm.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'smiled' + 'said' for two finished actions.

options: I pointed on a small brown bottle.
I pointed to small brown bottle.
I pointed at a small brown bottle.
I point at small brown bottle.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Point at + object' = use your finger to indicate. Past simple required.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'tone' mean in the context of Vietnamese pronunciation?
options: the volume of your voice
the language you speak
the colour of the word
the rising or falling pitch of a syllable that changes its meaning
mcQuestions.explanation: In tonal languages, the pitch contour ('tone') is part of the word's identity.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While I was paying, three other customers were listening.
While I paid, three other customers listened.
While I am paying, three other customers were listening.
While I pay, three other customers are listening.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous in both clauses shows two parallel ongoing actions.
mcQuestions.hint: Both actions ongoing = both past continuous.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence about being treated as a local.
options: People stopped to treat me like a tourist.
People stopped treating me like a tourist.
People stopped that they treat me like a tourist.
People are stopped treating me like a tourist.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stop + -ing' = stop the activity. 'Stop + to + verb' would mean stop in order to do something.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'regular' mean for a market customer?
options: a customer who pays in full
a customer who is polite
a customer who comes often
a customer from the same country

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'I started using the word ___ every other stall'.
options: on
in
by
at
mcQuestions.explanation: 'At a stall / at a counter' is the standard preposition for point locations in a market.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past simple of 'come'.
options: it came out flat
it comed out flat
it come out flat
it was come out flat

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly reports what was said?
options: She said it again, very slow, two syllables.
She said it again, very slowly, two syllables.
She said it again, very slowly, two syllable.
She say it again, very slowly, two syllables.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverb 'slowly' (not adjective 'slow') modifies 'said'. Plural 'syllables' is required.

options: I think she already know.
I think she yet knew.
I think she already knew.
I think she is already knew.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'knew' fits the past context; 'already' is naturally placed before the verb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why does learning one word in someone's language often change how they treat you more than memorising whole phrasebooks?
